title: rumex acetosella subsp. arenicola
intro: Rumex acetosella subsp. arenicola, also known as the mountain sorrel or mountain sheep's sorrel, is a subspecies of the sorrel plant native to North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its high content of oxalic acid and ascorbic acid, which have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rumex acetosella subsp. arenicola include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and improve overall immune function. Herbal preparations of Rumex acetosella subsp. arenicola can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and wounds.
